conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 8 years old. I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you some questions to assess your eligibility:

First, are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with him and are you responsible for paying for things like his food, clothes, and other needs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me at home and I take care of all his needs - food, clothes, school things, everything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming that.

Next question: Do you normally live in the UK? (This includes England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. We live in Bristol.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Let me ask a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for Alex, or is anyone else claiming him as part of their Child Benefit claim?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions:

Is Alex currently in hospital or in the care of the local authority (such as in foster care or a children's home)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, yes - Alex has been in hospital for a couple of months now. He had an accident and has needed quite a bit of treatment and recovery time. I visit him regularly and still buy him things he needs while he's there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to hear about Alex's accident. I hope he recovers well.

This is an important detail for Child Benefit eligibility. Let me ask: Do you expect Alex to be in hospital for more than 12 weeks in total, or will he likely come home before the 12-week mark?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: The doctors say he should be home before the 12-week mark, thankfully. He's been in there about 11 weeks now, so we're hoping he'll be discharged soon.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information, and I'm glad to hear Alex should be home soon.

Before I make my final assessment, let me just read the official guidance to make sure I give you accurate information about your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official guidance, I can now give you my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

✓ Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
✓ You are responsible for him - you live together and you pay for his needs
✓ You live in the UK (Bristol)
✓ Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him
✓ Although Alex has been in hospital for about 11 weeks, the rules only affect Child Benefit if a child is in hospital for **more than 12 weeks**. Since he's expected to come home before that point, his hospital stay doesn't disqualify you

You meet all the eligibility requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex. If you haven't already applied, you can do so through the official Child Benefit application process.

I hope Alex makes a full recovery and is home with you soon!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit); Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you normally, and you are responsible for him by providing for his needs. You live in the UK (Bristol), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Although Alex has been in hospital for approximately 11 weeks, the rules state that Child Benefit is only affected if a child is in hospital for more than 12 weeks. Since Alex is expected to be discharged before reaching the 12-week mark, his hospital stay does not affect your eligibility. You meet all the standard eligibility criteria: you are responsible for a child under 16, you normally live in the UK, and you are not disqualified by the hospital stay provisions.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They meet all the basic requirements: they are responsible for Alex, they live together in the UK, and no one else is claiming for him. Alex's current hospital stay of approximately 11 weeks does not disqualify the claim, as the rules only affect eligibility after 12 weeks in hospital.

Since Alex is expected to be discharged before reaching the 12-week mark, his hospital stay does not affect your eligibility. You meet all the standard eligibility criteria: you are responsible for a child under 16, you normally live in the UK, and you are not disqualified by the hospital stay provisions.

Alex's current hospital stay of approximately 11 weeks does not disqualify the claim, as the rules only affect eligibility after 12 weeks in hospital.
